concept: Content / Satisfied / At Peace with What Is
description: In a state of peaceful happiness and satisfaction. 'Nuloe' names the quality of contentment—the heart at rest, satisfied with what is present.
sound_symbolism: The word settles satisfied. 'Nu' grounds with quiet presence, needing nothing. 'Lo' flows with ease, desire released. Finally 'e' opens to acceptance, contentment complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'nuloe thaemo' (content heart). Related to 'thiro' (serene) but emphasizes satisfaction with circumstances rather than inner calm.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Contentment (santutthi) arises from releasing craving—satisfaction with what is present rather than grasping for more.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Content communities thrived—satisfaction with enough enabled sustainable living.
